chunk_summary: Guidelines for safe preparation of human milk fortifiers (HMF) and osmolality management to ensure nutritional safety and minimize contamination risks in expressed and donor milk handling.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of sterile preparation and handling of human milk fortifiers (HMF).
Learn strategies to manage osmolality in fortified human milk to reduce gastrointestinal risks.
Identify time and temperature guidelines for safe storage and use of fortified milk.
Recognize contamination risks and mitigation strategies in milk preparation and feeding.
key_facts: Powdered HMF products are not sterile and should be prepared in a clean area with proper hygiene practices.
Fortified milk should be refrigerated promptly and used within 24 hours; discard if held at room temperature beyond unit limits.
Continuous tube feeds should have a hang time limit of 4 hours, with syringes and tubing changed on schedule.
Fortified feeds should ideally remain below 450 mOsm/kg to minimize gastrointestinal injury risk.
Osmolality can increase over time after mixing, especially with multiple modulars like protein and electrolytes.
Sterile liquid fortifiers reduce contamination risk but may be cost-prohibitive in certain regions like India.
reference_quote: Fortification of human milk for preterm infants: update and recommendations of the European Milk Bank Association Working Group — https://pmc.ncbi.nlm...
document_key_topics: Safe preparation of human milk fortifiers
Storage and feeding guidelines for fortified milk
